---
title: "Data Collection_Final Project"
author: "Ruiyu Li"
date: "2026-04-24"
output: html_document
---

#1.Loading Data
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

raw <- read.csv("../data/Time Spent on Short-video Platforms and Self-reported Difficulty on Academic Work Among University Students.csv",
                stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
                check.names = FALSE,
                encoding = "UTF-8",
                na.strings = c("", "NA"))

names(raw) <- c("timestamp", "consent", "gender", "age", "year",
                "field", "adhd", "use_sv", "platforms", "sv_time",
                "sv_when", "task_type", "sv_interrupt",
                "concentration", "long_read", "phone_check",
                "return_difficulty", "open_comment")
```



#2.Clean Data
```{r}
# 2.1 Rename columns to short names
names(raw) <- c("timestamp", "consent", "gender", "age", "year",
                "field", "adhd", "use_sv", "platforms", "sv_time",
                "sv_when", "task_type", "sv_interrupt",
                "concentration", "long_read", "phone_check",
                "return_difficulty", "open_comment")

raw <- raw %>% mutate(across(where(is.character), trimws))

# 2.2 Filter: only people who use short-video
df <- raw %>% filter(use_sv == "Yes")
cat("After filtering non-users: n =", nrow(df), "\n")

# 2.3 Convert IV to ordered factor
df$sv_time <- factor(df$sv_time,
                     levels = c("Less than 30 minutes",
                                "30 minutes to 1 hour",
                                "1 to 2 hours",
                                "2 to 3 hours",
                                "More than 3 hours"),
                     ordered = TRUE)
df$sv_time_num <- as.numeric(df$sv_time)

# 2.4 Complete case analysis
n_before <- nrow(df)
df <- df %>% filter(!is.na(concentration), !is.na(sv_time))
n_after <- nrow(df)
cat("Excluded due to missing IV/DV:", n_before - n_after, "\n")
cat("Final n =", n_after, "\n")

# 2.5 Clean field: merge small categories
df$field_clean <- ifelse(df$field == "Accounting and Finance",
                         "Business & Management",
                         df$field)

cat("\nField distribution:\n")
print(table(df$field_clean, useNA = "always"))

# 2.6 Final sanity check
cat("\n--- Final columns in df ---\n")
print(names(df))

cat("IV (sv_time) distribution:\n")
print(table(df$sv_time))

cat("\nDV (concentration) distribution:\n")
print(table(df$concentration))

cat("\nField (after merging) distribution:\n")
print(table(df$field_clean))
```

#3.Descriptive Statistics
```{r}
# 3.1 Sample characteristics
cat("\n========== SAMPLE CHARACTERISTICS ==========\n")
cat("N =", nrow(df), "\n\n")

cat("--- Gender ---\n")
print(table(df$gender))
print(round(prop.table(table(df$gender)) * 100, 1))

cat("\n--- Age ---\n")
print(table(df$age))

cat("\n--- Year of study ---\n")
print(table(df$year))

cat("\n--- Field of study (after merging) ---\n")
print(table(df$field_clean))

cat("\n--- ADHD diagnosis ---\n")
print(table(df$adhd))

# 3.2 IV distribution
cat("\n--- IV: Daily short-video time ---\n")
iv_dist <- df %>%
  count(sv_time) %>%
  mutate(pct = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 1))
print(iv_dist)

# 3.3 DV descriptive stats 
cat("\n--- DV: Concentration difficulty ---\n")
cat("Median =", median(df$concentration), "\n")
cat("IQR =", IQR(df$concentration), "\n") 
cat("Distribution:\n")
print(table(df$concentration))

# 3.4 Visualise IV distribution 
p_iv <- ggplot(df, aes(x = sv_time)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "steelblue") +
  labs(x = "Daily short-video time",
       y = "Count",
       title = paste0("Distribution of daily short-video use (n=", nrow(df), ")")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1))

ggsave("outputs/fig1_iv_distribution.png", p_iv,
       width = 7, height = 4.5, dpi = 300)

# 3.5 Visualise DV distribution
p_dv <- ggplot(df, aes(x = factor(concentration))) +
  geom_bar(fill = "coral") +
  labs(x = "Concentration difficulty (1 = Strongly disagree, 5 = Strongly agree)",
       y = "Count",
       title = "Distribution of self-reported concentration difficulty") +
  theme_minimal()

ggsave("outputs/fig2_dv_distribution.png", p_dv,
       width = 7, height = 4.5, dpi = 300)
```



#4.H1 — Spearman's Rank-Order Correlation
```{r}
cat("\n========== H1: SPEARMAN CORRELATION ==========\n")

# 4.1 Run the test 
spearman_result <- cor.test(df$sv_time_num, df$concentration,
                            method = "spearman",
                            alternative = "two.sided",
                            exact = FALSE)
print(spearman_result)

# 4.2 Extract key numbers for APA reporting 
rs <- round(spearman_result$estimate, 3)
p_val <- spearman_result$p.value
p_val_fmt <- ifelse(p_val < .001, "< .001",
                    paste0("= ", sprintf("%.3f", p_val)))

cat("\n--- APA-style report ---\n")
cat("Spearman's rs =", rs, ", p ", p_val_fmt,
    ", n =", nrow(df), "\n")

# Cohen's guideline
effect_label <- ifelse(abs(rs) < 0.10, "negligible",
                ifelse(abs(rs) < 0.30, "weak",
                ifelse(abs(rs) < 0.50, "moderate", "strong")))
cat("Effect size interpretation:", effect_label, "\n")

# 4.3 Visualise H1 — jittered scatter 
p_h1a <- ggplot(df, aes(x = sv_time_num, y = concentration)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0.2, alpha = 0.5, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "darkred") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:5,
                     labels = c("<30m", "30m-1h", "1-2h", "2-3h", ">3h")) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = 1:5) +
  labs(x = "Daily short-video time",
       y = "Concentration difficulty",
       title = paste0("Spearman's rs = ", rs, ", p ", p_val_fmt)) +
  theme_minimal()

ggsave("outputs/fig3_h1_scatter.png", p_h1a,
       width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)

# 4.4 Visualise H1 — boxplot by usage level 
p_h1b <- ggplot(df, aes(x = sv_time, y = concentration)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightblue") +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.4) +
  labs(x = "Daily short-video time",
       y = "Concentration difficulty") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1))

ggsave("outputs/fig3_h1_boxplot.png", p_h1b,
       width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```


#5.H2 — Mann-Whitney U Test (High vs Low usage)
```{r}
cat("\n========== H2: MANN-WHITNEY U TEST ==========\n")

# 5.1 Build High vs Low groups
# Low: <30min + 30m-1h
# High: 2-3h + >3h
df_h2 <- df %>%
  mutate(usage_group = case_when(
    sv_time %in% c("Less than 30 minutes", "30 minutes to 1 hour") ~ "Low",
    sv_time %in% c("2 to 3 hours", "More than 3 hours") ~ "High",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  )) %>%
  filter(!is.na(usage_group)) %>%
  mutate(usage_group = factor(usage_group, levels = c("Low", "High")))

cat("H2 sample distribution:\n")
print(table(df_h2$usage_group))

# 5.2 Run Mann-Whitney U test
mw_result <- wilcox.test(concentration ~ usage_group,
                         data = df_h2,
                         alternative = "two.sided",
                         exact = FALSE,       
                         correct = TRUE)      
print(mw_result)

# 5.3 Calculate descriptives for each group
cat("\n--- Group descriptives ---\n")
med_stats <- df_h2 %>%
  group_by(usage_group) %>%
  summarise(n = n(),
            median = median(concentration),
            IQR = IQR(concentration),
            .groups = "drop")
print(med_stats)

# 5.4 Visualise H2
p_h2 <- ggplot(df_h2, aes(x = usage_group, y = concentration, fill = usage_group)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(x = "Usage group",
       y = "Concentration difficulty",
       title = "Concentration difficulty by usage group") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

ggsave("outputs/fig4_h2_boxplot.png", p_h2,
       width = 6, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

#6.H3 — Exploratory Analyses
```{r}
cat("\n========== H3: EXPLORATORY ANALYSES ==========\n")

# 6.1 Gender comparison (Mann-Whitney U) 
cat("\n--- 6.1 Gender (Mann-Whitney U) ---\n")

gender_mw <- wilcox.test(concentration ~ gender,
                         data = df,
                         alternative = "two.sided",
                         exact = FALSE)
print(gender_mw)

gender_desc <- df %>%
  group_by(gender) %>%
  summarise(n = n(),
            median = median(concentration),
            IQR = IQR(concentration),
            .groups = "drop")
print(gender_desc)

# Visualise
p_h3_gender <- ggplot(df, aes(x = gender, y = concentration, fill = gender)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(x = "Gender",
       y = "Concentration difficulty",
       title = "Concentration difficulty by gender") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

ggsave("outputs/fig5_h3_gender.png", p_h3_gender,
       width = 6, height = 5, dpi = 300)

# 6.2 ADHD 
cat("\n--- 6.2 ADHD diagnosis ---\n")

adhd_desc <- df %>%
  group_by(adhd) %>%
  summarise(n = n(),
            median = median(concentration),
            IQR = IQR(concentration),
            .groups = "drop")
print(adhd_desc)

cat("Note: Inferential testing not feasible due to small subgroup sizes.\n")

# 6.3 Field of study (Kruskal-Wallis) 
cat("\n--- 6.3 Field of study (Kruskal-Wallis) ---\n")

df$field_clean <- factor(df$field_clean,
                         levels = c("Arts & Humanities",
                                    "Business & Management",
                                    "Social Sciences",
                                    "Natural Sciences & Engineering"))

kw_field <- kruskal.test(concentration ~ field_clean, data = df)
print(kw_field)

field_desc <- df %>%
  group_by(field_clean) %>%
  summarise(n = n(),
            median = median(concentration),
            IQR = IQR(concentration),
            .groups = "drop")
print(field_desc)

if (kw_field$p.value < 0.05) {
  cat("\nKW is significant. Compare group medians above.\n")
} else {
  cat("\nKW is not significant.\n")
}

# Visualise
p_h3_field <- ggplot(df, aes(x = field_clean, y = concentration, fill = field_clean)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(x = "Field of study",
       y = "Concentration difficulty",
       title = "Concentration difficulty by field of study") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1))

ggsave("outputs/fig6_h3_field.png", p_h3_field,
       width = 7.5, height = 5, dpi = 300)

# 6.4 Task type (Kruskal-Wallis) 
cat("\n--- 6.4 Task type (Kruskal-Wallis) ---\n")

df_task <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(task_type)) %>%
  mutate(task_type_merged = case_when(
    task_type %in% c("Lab or practical work", "Problem sets") ~ "Other technical work",
    TRUE ~ task_type
  ))

df_task$task_type_merged <- factor(df_task$task_type_merged,
                                    levels = c("Lecture attendance",
                                               "Reading (journal articles, textbooks)",
                                               "Writing (essays, dissertations)",
                                               "Other technical work"))

cat("Merged task_type distribution:\n")
print(table(df_task$task_type_merged))

kw_task <- kruskal.test(concentration ~ task_type_merged, data = df_task)
print(kw_task)

if (kw_task$p.value < 0.05) {
  cat("\nKW is significant. Compare group medians above to identify which task type(s) differ.\n")
} else {
  cat("\nKW is not significant — concentration difficulty does not vary meaningfully by task type.\n")
}

# Visualise
p_h3_task <- ggplot(df_task, aes(x = task_type_merged, y = concentration, fill = task_type_merged)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(x = "Primary academic task",
       y = "Concentration difficulty",
       title = "Concentration difficulty by primary academic task") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1))

ggsave("outputs/fig7_h3_task.png", p_h3_task,
       width = 7.5, height = 5, dpi = 300)

```
#7.Save Results 
```{r}
sink("outputs/all_results.txt")

cat("=============================================\n")
cat("FULL RESULTS — Short-video & Concentration\n")
cat("Analytic sample: n =", nrow(df), "\n")
cat("=============================================\n\n")

cat("=== H1: Spearman's Rank-Order Correlation ===\n")
print(spearman_result)

cat("\n=== H2: Mann-Whitney U (High vs Low usage) ===\n")
print(mw_result)

cat("\n=== H3.1: Mann-Whitney U (Gender) ===\n")
print(gender_mw)
print(gender_desc)

cat("\n=== H3.2: ADHD (descriptive only) ===\n")
print(adhd_desc)

cat("\n=== H3.3: Kruskal-Wallis (Field) ===\n")
print(kw_field)
print(field_desc)

cat("\n=== H3.4: Kruskal-Wallis (Task type) ===\n")
print(kw_task)
print(task_desc)

sink()

cat("\n✓ All results saved to outputs/all_results.txt\n\n")


# R package citations
cat("========== PACKAGE CITATIONS ==========\n")
cat("\n--- R (base) ---\n"); print(citation())
cat("\n--- dplyr ---\n"); print(citation("dplyr"))
cat("\n--- ggplot2 ---\n"); print(citation("ggplot2"))
cat("\n--- tidyr ---\n"); print(citation("tidyr"))
```
